a: Rhobear Chat Brain checks a semantic cache first. If your question closely matches a cached answer (cosine similarity >= 0.86), you get an instant response. Otherwise it forwards to a local llama.cpp model.
thought: Let me explain the gateway flow.

a: Cache hits are served from our semantic cache. On cache miss, requests are forwarded to a local llama.cpp server running an open-weight model on CPU.
thought: Checking whether this is a cache hit or upstream call.

q: What's the cost to run this?
a: The target deployment is about $0.06/hour on a small CPU VPS. Semantic caching keeps most demo traffic off the LLM, so cost stays near zero for repeated questions.
Yes. Rhobear Chat Brain is open source. You can self-host the gateway, sqlite-vec cache, and llama.cpp upstream.
It is an OpenAI-compatible chat gateway with a semantic cache backed by sqlite-vec. It makes a cheap CPU box feel fast for common questions.
Embeddings let us match paraphrased questions to cached answers. That avoids running the LLM for repetitive support and sales FAQs.

q: What embedding model powers the cache?
a: We use BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 via sentence-transformers on CPU. It embeds a query in about 30 ms on a c4-class box.

q: What vector database do you use?
a: sqlite-vec in a single SQLite file at ./data/cache.db. No Redis, no external vector service.

q: What similarity threshold triggers a cache hit?
a: Default CACHE_THRESHOLD is 0.86 cosine similarity. You can tune it with an environment variable.
Yes. POST /v1/chat/completions supports stream: true. Cache hits fake-stream at about 40 tokens per second; misses proxy the upstream SSE stream.

q: What happens on a cache miss?
a: The gateway forwards the request to llama.cpp at LLAMA_UPSTREAM (default http://localhost:8080) and proxies the response back unchanged.
Seeded FAQ questions typically respond in under 200 ms because embedding plus sqlite-vec lookup is lightweight on CPU.

q: Can I add new cached answers without redeploying?
a: Yes. POST newline-delimited JSON to /admin/seed with the X-Admin-Token header. Seeding is idempotent by question hash.
GET /metrics.json returns p50_ms, p95_ms, tok_per_sec, cache_hit_rate, requests_total, and cost_per_1k_estimate from the last 24 hours.
Every chat request appends a row to ./data/requests.db with timestamp, prompt hash, hit/miss, similarity, latency, tokens out, and model used.

q: What is the health check endpoint?
a: GET /healthz returns 200 when sqlite cache, request log, and llama.cpp upstream are all reachable.

q: What port does the service listen on?
a: Default PORT is 8000. Configure it with the PORT environment variable.
Yes. Clients can point at /v1/chat/completions with the usual messages format and optional stream flag.
No GPU is required. Embeddings and sqlite-vec run on CPU, and llama.cpp serves the miss path locally.

q: What language is the service written in?
a: Python 3.11 with FastAPI and uvicorn.

q: How are seeds loaded on first boot?
a: The service reads seeds/sales-faq.jsonl, embeds each question, and inserts rows into sqlite-vec unless the question hash already exists.

q: What is in the demo seed file?
a: sales-faq.jsonl ships with 30 placeholder Q&A pairs about this chatbot demo. Replace them with your own product FAQs later.

q: Can the cache store optional thinking text?
a: Yes. Seed entries may include an optional thought field that is shown before the answer on cache hits.

q: How do I run it locally?
a: Install requirements, set ADMIN_TOKEN, start llama.cpp on port 8080, then run uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000.
Yes. The repo includes a multi-stage Dockerfile targeting an image under 800 MB with the embedding model baked in.
Yes. systemd/chat-brain.service runs the app as user chatbrain from /opt/chat-brain with Restart=always.
A local llama.cpp OpenAI-compatible server, defaulting to http://localhost:8080/v1/chat/completions.
We hash the latest user message with SHA-256 for logging. Cache matching uses embedding similarity, not exact string match.
Teams that want an instant-feeling support bot on a $0.06/hr CPU box without paying per-token cloud inference for every repeat question.

q: Where is the cache stored on disk?
a: Vector index and cached answers live in ./data/cache.db. Request metrics live separately in ./data/requests.db.
